posts.title: Grey Structure Construction Lahore — Complete Guide
posts.summary: What is grey structure, what work is included from foundation to steel, concrete, walls and floor — complete guide by Mhbuilder Lahore.
posts.body:
What is Grey Structure?

Grey structure is the basic skeleton of any house or commercial building that is completed before all finishing work. It includes everything from foundation to roof. This framework bears the entire load of the building, so it must be strong and built according to standard specifications.

1. Excavation and Foundation

The first and most important step of grey structure is excavation. After a soil test, the foundation design is prepared. In Lahore, strip foundation or raft foundation is commonly used. First, crush (gravel) is laid, then steel binding is done, and finally concrete is poured. The foundation is the base of the entire building — if it is weak, the whole house is at risk. At Mhbuilder Lahore, we follow the drawing plan strictly for foundation depth and width with zero compromise.

2. RCC Columns and Beams

After the foundation, RCC (Reinforced Cement Concrete) columns are erected. Steel rods (rebar) are placed inside the columns to give tensile strength to the structure. The quantity and diameter of steel follows the structural engineer's drawing. Beams are also cast alongside columns to connect them and support the floor slab. Only approved grade steel (ASTM A615 or BS 4449) should be used. At Mhbuilder Lahore, we personally inspect every column's steel binding before proceeding.

3. Brick Masonry and Walls

After columns, brick masonry begins. Standard size burnt clay bricks are mostly used in Lahore. Walls are of two types — 9 inch (load bearing) and 4.5 inch (partition walls). The mortar mix (cement, sand, water) must be in the correct ratio — usually 1:6 or 1:4. Walls must be perfectly plumb and level, otherwise plastering problems arise later. Horizontal steel (lintels) must be placed above every opening after every 3 courses.

4. Roof Slab and Stairs

After walls are complete, roof slab casting takes place. First shuttering is installed (wooden or steel), then steel binding is done, and finally concrete is poured. Roof slab thickness is usually 5 to 6 inches depending on the span. Concrete mix should be M20 or stronger. After casting, curing (watering) for at least 21 days is essential — this is the most critical step that people often skip. Stairs are also cast at this stage.

5. Floor Concrete

Each floor is first filled with gravel, then compacted, and a layer of plain cement concrete (PCC) is applied on top. This layer becomes the base for tiles or marble. The floor must be perfectly level — even one inch difference causes major problems later. At Mhbuilder Lahore, we check with a spirit level before approving floor concrete.

6. Electrical and Plumbing Conduits

During grey structure, PVC conduit pipes are embedded inside walls and roof for electrical wiring. Similarly, water supply pipes and drainage pipes are also fitted during grey structure. Doing this work later is very expensive and difficult, so it must be done during grey structure. At Mhbuilder Lahore, we coordinate with electrical and plumbing contractors to complete this work during grey structure stage.

Time to Complete Grey Structure

Grey structure of a 5 marla house is generally completed in 3 to 5 months. A 10 marla or larger house can take 6 to 9 months. This timeline also depends on the number of workers, material availability and weather conditions.

Important Tips

  • Use only approved and tested cement (Lucky, Maple Leaf, DG Khan)
  • Water must be clean — brackish water weakens concrete
  • Curing for 21 days after concrete casting is mandatory
  • Never compromise on structural drawings
  • Get every stage inspected by yourself or a trusted engineer
